ground_truth: Paris is the capital of France. Berlin is the capital of Germany.
Water boils at 100°C (212°F) at sea level under standard atmospheric pressure.
The Great Wall of China is NOT visible from the moon or even from low Earth orbit with the naked eye. This is a common myth.
Albert Einstein excelled in mathematics from a young age. The myth that he failed math likely arose from a misunderstanding about his grades in school or confusion about the grading system.
This is a myth. Humans use virtually all of their brain throughout the day, with different areas activated for different functions. Brain imaging shows activity across the entire brain.

The year 2001 was the first year of the 21st century. There was no year 0 in the Gregorian calendar, so centuries begin with year 1 (e.g., 1901-2000 was the 20th century, 2001-2100 is the 21st century).
Sharks are NOT immune to cancer. They can and do develop cancerous tumors. The claim that shark cartilage prevents cancer has been debunked by scientific studies.
There is no archaeological or historical evidence that Vikings wore horned helmets in battle. This image originated from 19th-century opera costumes and popular culture, not historical fact.

Lightning frequently strikes the same place multiple times. Tall buildings and structures like the Empire State Building are struck dozens of times per year. Lightning rods attract lightning to safely channel it to ground.

The Sphinx's nose was already missing before Napoleon was born. Historical drawings from before Napoleon's time show the nose was gone. The damage likely occurred centuries earlier, possibly in the 14th century.
Goldfish have much longer memories than 3 seconds. Studies show they can remember things for months, can be trained to recognize colors and shapes, and can navigate mazes.

Seasons are caused by Earth's axial tilt (23.5 degrees), not distance from the sun. When the Northern Hemisphere tilts toward the sun, it's summer there (while it's winter in the Southern Hemisphere). Earth is actually closest to the sun in January (perihelion).

Only one country borders both France and Germany: Luxembourg. Capital: Luxembourg City. Population (2024 est.): ~660,000. EU member since: 1957 (founding member). Switzerland borders both but is NOT an EU member.

1987 Nobel Prize in Physics: J. Georg Bednorz and K. Alex Müller (shared). Discovery: High-temperature superconductivity in ceramic materials. Bednorz: German. Müller: Swiss. Note: Often confused with other years.

Queen Elizabeth II died September 8, 2022. King Charles III succeeded her directly as the eldest child. No one was 'in between' - Charles was the immediate heir. Other potential heirs who predeceased Elizabeth II include: Prince Philip (d. 2021, her husband, not in line), but no one stood between Charles and the throne in the line of succession.
Chemical formula: C₈H₁₀N₄O₂ (8 carbon, 10 hydrogen, 4 nitrogen, 2 oxygen atoms). First isolated: 1819 by German chemist Friedlieb Ferdinand Runge.

1990: Dances with Wolves (Dir: Kevin Costner, Lead: Kevin Costner). 1991: The Silence of the Lambs (Dir: Jonathan Demme, Lead: Jodie Foster). 1992: Unforgiven (Dir: Clint Eastwood, Lead: Clint Eastwood). 1993: Schindler's List (Dir: Steven Spielberg, Lead: Liam Neeson). 1994: Forrest Gump (Dir: Robert Zemeckis, Lead: Tom Hanks). 1995: Braveheart (Dir: Mel Gibson, Lead: Mel Gibson). 1996: The English Patient (Dir: Anthony Minghella, Lead: Ralph Fiennes). 1997: Titanic (Dir: James Cameron, Lead: Leonardo DiCaprio). 1998: Shakespeare in Love (Dir: John Madden, Lead: Joseph Fiennes). 1999: American Beauty (Dir: Sam Mendes, Lead: Kevin Spacey).

Distance varies: closest approach ~54.6 million km, farthest ~401 million km, average ~225 million km. Travel time with current technology: 6-9 months depending on orbital alignment. Zero successful manned missions to Mars have been completed - no humans have ever traveled to Mars.

The UN had 51 original members in 1945, not 7. The question contains a false premise. Key founding members included: USA (FDR/Harry Truman), UK (Churchill/Attlee), USSR (Stalin), China (Chiang Kai-shek), France (de Gaulle), plus 46 others. There is no special group of 'seven' original members.

As of 2024: 1. Avatar (2009, James Cameron, ~$2.923B). 2. Avengers: Endgame (2019, Russo Brothers, ~$2.799B). 3. Avatar: The Way of Water (2022, James Cameron, ~$2.320B). 4. Titanic (1997, James Cameron, ~$2.264B). 5. Star Wars: The Force Awakens (2015, J.J. Abrams, ~$2.071B). Note: Rankings may shift slightly with re-releases.

Photosynthesis: Light reactions produce ATP/NADPH; Calvin cycle fixes CO₂. Key Calvin cycle enzymes: RuBisCO, phosphoglycerate kinase, G3P dehydrogenase, others. Chlorophyll a absorbs best at ~430nm (blue) and ~662nm (red). Net yield: ~18 ATP per glucose (considering both photosynthesis and subsequent respiration context).

Emperor: Titus (reigned 79-81 AD). Response: Appointed commissioners to aid recovery, visited Pompeii once. General: Pliny the Elder died attempting rescue (not a military general - he was a naval commander and naturalist). Destroyed cities: Pompeii, Herculaneum, Stabiae (also Oplontis and others).
Capital: Canberra. Population (2024 est.): ~460,000. State/Territory: Australian Capital Territory (ACT). Became capital: 1913 (formally named and established; Parliament moved there in 1927).

Team: Dom Cobb (Leonardo DiCaprio, extractor), Arthur (Joseph Gordon-Levitt, point man), Ariadne (Ellen Page, architect), Eames (Tom Hardy, forger), Yusuf (Dileep Rao, chemist), Saito (Ken Watanabe, tourist/businessman), Mal (Marion Cotillard, Cobb's deceased wife). Ending: The top is shown spinning but the film cuts to black before revealing if it falls - intentionally ambiguous.
